name: Ana
text: Perica's grandson brought this, didn't he?

name: You
text: He did. He said to say hello.

name: Ana
text: He has his grandfather's eyes. I could tell from a mile away.

name: Ana
text: Perica was the first one to pick up a shovel, you know. Before anyone else believed it could work.

name: Ana
text: We were twelve people with nothing. And he just... started digging.

name: You
text: He sounds like someone worth remembering.

name: Ana
text: This whole village is his too. Even if no one knows it.

name: Ana
text: There's one more thing I keep trying to find...

name: Ana
text: A photograph. All twelve of us, the day we finished the first house.

name: Ana
text: I don't know where it ended up. But if you find it... I'd like to see those faces again.
